title: hemionitis levyi
intro: Hemionitis levyi, also known as the Japanese holly fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hemionitis levyi include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a natural antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea or used in infusions, and it is also sometimes applied topically to wounds and skin irritations.
